sections.paragraph: Isa 21:14 Jer 25:23 and תֵּמָא Job 6:19 (i.q. تَيْمَاَءُ “a desert,” “an untilled district”), [Tema], pr.n. of a country and nation in the northern part of Arabia Deserts, on the borders of the desert of Syria; the name comes from Thema, the son of Ishmael ( Gen 25:15); now called by the Arabs تيما. The LXX. every where write the word Θαιμάν, as though it had been the same as תֵּימָן.
